MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant City And County Of San Francisco And Andre Stephen Richardsons
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. RANDALL H. SCARLETT and BRENDAN D.S. WAY of Scarlett Law Group, Tel No. (415) 352-6264 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. KAREN E. KIRBY of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. (415) 554-3800 appearing for Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Andre Stephen Richardson;
Atty. LANDA LOW of the California Department of Transportation, Tel No. (510) 433-9100 appearing for Defendant State of California, California Department of Transportation ("Caltrans").

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ANDRE STEPHEN RICHARDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, the court denies the motion for summary adjudication. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production per CCP 437c. Gov't Code secs. 830.4 and 830.8 do not bar this action because lack of signage/signals is merely one component of the alleged defective condition of public property. Defendant fails to show that the design immunity defense of Gov't Code sec. 830.6 applies. The Woo declaration does not sufficiently establish discretionary approval of the design, including Cal Trans' decision to use a crosswalk with a 50 degree angle skew. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the defective design caused the accident. There is also a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant had actual and/or constructive notice of the alleged defects. There was a prior similar accident at the intersection and defendant's 19th Ave. Corridor study noted some of the same cited defects. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. RANDALL H. SCARLETT and BRENDAN D.S. WAY of Scarlett Law Group, Tel No. (415) 352-6264 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. KAREN E. KIRBY of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. (415) 554-3800 appearing for Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Andre Stephen Richardson;
Atty. LANDA LOW of the California Department of Transportation, Tel No. (510) 433-9100 appearing for Defendant State of California, California Department of Transportation ("Caltrans").

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION ("CALTRANS")'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production pursuant to CCP 437c. Plaintiff alleges, inter alia, that the crosswalk was a dangerous condition of public property at par. 33 of her complaint. Defendant argues that the crosswalk was not a dangerous condition, but cites no evidence to support that assertion. In addition, the existence of a dangerous condition of public property is ordinarily a question of fact. In Bonanno v. Central Contra Costa Transit Authority (2003) 30 Cal.4th 139, the court explains that hazards present on adjoining property may create a dangerous condition of public property when users of the public property are necessarily exposed to those risks. Here, there is a disputed issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's crosswalk in combination with the other factors created a dangerous condition of public property. At paragraph 17 of his declaration, plaintiff's expert (Matthew Manharrez) explains that the skew angle of the crosswalk oriented plaintiff away from the approaching LRV and such a design decreased pedestrian visibility. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LANDA LOW, DEPUTY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 904-5700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained, with leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot allege a negligence cause of action against Defendant Caltrans. "In California all government tort liability is now dependent on statute." (Susman v. City of Los Angeles (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 803, 808.) "Under the [Government Claims] Act, there is no common law liability of a public entity; liability is wholly statutory." (4 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Plead, 617, p. 748; see Gov. Code 815.) Statutory causes of action must be specifically pled, and every element of the statutory basis for liability must be specially alleged. (Ibid.) Plaintiff is granted 10 days leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith, to specially plead a statutory cause of action against Defendant Caltrans. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
